outcomes.headline: Drive-through pacing held under 8 minutes
outcomes.body: Client wait stayed under 8 minutes after the first half hour. The pre-event sort meaningfully reduced setup time and is worth keeping as a standing procedure.

outcomes.headline: Two interns ran the produce table unsupervised
outcomes.body: Engineering interns Janelle and Reza paired with regular Saturday volunteers and were running the table independently within 30 minutes. A low-friction onboarding pattern worth modeling for new hires.

outcomes.headline: Mobility access is not solved
outcomes.body: One volunteer had to switch roles mid-event because the warehouse-to-staging route isn't wheelchair-friendly. We didn't ask on the registration form, which is a fix for next quarter.

recommendations.headline: Add mobility question to registration
recommendations.body: A single line on the form would have surfaced the issue before Saturday morning and given us time to plan around it. Add to the standard event template now.

recommendations.headline: Order supplies against prior-event consumption, not headcount
recommendations.body: We ordered for headcount × 2 and shipped 40 unused boxes back to the warehouse. Past three events suggest 1.2× consumption is the right multiplier; update the order template.

recommendations.headline: Repeat the Friday pre-sort
recommendations.body: It cut Saturday setup by 35 minutes and paid for itself in volunteer hours saved. Make it a standing operating procedure for any warehouse-side event.
